name: Varuna Extended
description: Varuna, in Hindu mythology, is a powerful and complex deity. Initially, he was considered the supreme celestial god, the upholder of cosmic order (Rta), and the guardian of moral law. He is depicted as the king of the gods, a position he later ceded to Indra. Varuna is associated with the sky, water, and justice. He possesses the power to bind and release, both physically and morally. His all-seeing eyes, often represented by the sun and stars, allow him to observe all actions and thoughts, making him a judge of immense authority. He uses his noose (Pasha) to ensnare wrongdoers and enforce divine justice. While his prominence diminished over time, he remains a significant figure, representing the importance of righteousness and the consequences of violating cosmic and moral laws. He is also linked to the ocean depths, controlling the waters and the creatures within. His influence extends to oaths, contracts, and the maintenance of truth in the universe. He is often invoked alongside Mitra, representing a dualistic concept of divine authority. Varuna's role evolves from a supreme ruler to a powerful guardian of specific domains, particularly water and justice, retaining his significance within the Hindu pantheon as a reminder of the importance of adhering to dharma.
subtitle: Guardian of Cosmic Order and Justice

keyMyths.title: Varuna and the Binding of Vritra
keyMyths.description: While Indra is credited with slaying Vritra, Varuna's role in binding the demon initially is also significant, highlighting his power over imprisonment and control.

keyMyths.title: Varuna's All-Seeing Eyes
keyMyths.description: The myth emphasizes Varuna's omniscience, highlighting that no actions, even those hidden, escape his gaze, reinforcing his role as a moral judge.

keyMyths.title: Varuna and Harishchandra
keyMyths.description: King Harishchandra's broken promise to Varuna leads to a series of trials, demonstrating the consequences of violating oaths and the importance of truthfulness.

sources.author: Wendy Doniger
sources.description: Provides an overview of Varuna's role and evolution in Hindu mythology.

sources.author: A.A. Macdonell
